appeal_text: We request first-level Humana Medicare Advantage organization-determination reconsideration of the denial of semaglutide (Ozempic) for our patient with type 2 diabetes with hyperglycemia and established atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease (ICD-10 E11.65 and I25.10). Updated documentation supplies the full step-therapy criterion required by Humana's Medical and Pharmacy Coverage Policy on GLP-1 Receptor Agonists for Type 2 Diabetes: (1) A1C 8.4% on 2026-04-12 (within 90-day window); (2) metformin titration history: started at 500 mg BID 2024-09, titrated to 1000 mg BID by 2024-11, escalated to 2000 mg total daily over 4 weeks (well-tolerated, eGFR 76 mL/min throughout), maintained through 2025-12 with documented adherence by pharmacy fill records and A1C trend 7.8 → 7.9 → 8.2 → 8.4 (loss of glycemic control on maximally tolerated metformin); (3) sulfonylurea (glipizide 10 mg BID) trial 2025-06 through 2025-09 discontinued for two documented hypoglycemic events (BG 52 and 48 mg/dL with symptoms); (4) the patient has established ASCVD (prior MI 2023-08, on aspirin/atorvastatin/lisinopril), placing the patient in the ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026 elevated-priority phenotype for GLP-1 receptor agonist therapy independent of A1C target. Submitted within the 65-day Medicare Advantage timely-filing window. Attached: A1C trend, pharmacy fill records, hypoglycemia documentation, CV history, current medication list.
citations: Humana Medical and Pharmacy Coverage Policy: GLP-1 Receptor Agonists for Type 2 Diabetes
ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026: Summary of Revisions
FDA-Approved Prescribing Information: Ozempic (semaglutide injection)

appeal_text: We request first-level commercial reconsideration of the denial of semaglutide (Wegovy) for our patient with class 2 obesity (ICD-10 E66.812, BMI 38.4) and obstructive sleep apnea (ICD-10 G47.33) under a Humana commercial (non-Part-D) plan. Updated documentation: (1) BMI 38.4 confirmed at clinic on 2026-04-22, exceeding the ≥30 baseline; (2) comorbidities include moderate-severe OSA on PSG 2026-02-14 (AHI 32), prediabetes (A1C 6.2), and hypertension on lisinopril/amlodipine; (3) structured lifestyle program documented: completed 12-week medically supervised intensive behavioral therapy 2026-01 through 2026-03 with attached attendance/progress log; weight trajectory 105 kg → 102 kg → 101 kg (modest non-sustained loss); (4) registered dietitian quarterly visits documented; (5) the patient is enrolled in a Humana commercial plan (group ID attached), and per Humana's Medical and Pharmacy Coverage Policy on Anti-Obesity GLP-1 Receptor Agonists for commercial plans (which differ from Part-D structural exclusions), Wegovy is covered with PA. The FDA-approved Wegovy labeling supports use for chronic weight management in patients with BMI ≥30 or BMI ≥27 with comorbidity. Submitted within the 180-day commercial window. Attached: BMI trend, PSG, lifestyle-program records, dietitian notes.
citations: Humana Medical and Pharmacy Coverage Policy: Anti-Obesity GLP-1 Receptor Agonists
FDA-Approved Prescribing Information: Wegovy (semaglutide injection 2.4 mg)
Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline: Pharmacological Management of Obesity

appeal_text: We request first-level Humana Medicare Advantage organization-determination reconsideration of the denial of an external insulin infusion pump (HCPCS E0784) for our patient with type 1 diabetes mellitus with hyperglycemia (ICD-10 E10.65). Updated documentation supplies the full CMS-aligned criterion: (1) C-peptide < 0.5 ng/mL on 2026-03-04 with concurrent glucose 198 mg/dL, confirming insulinopenia; (2) completed diabetes self-management education over 6 sessions 2026-01 through 2026-02 (training documentation attached); (3) self-monitoring blood glucose log demonstrating 5-7 daily measurements over 30 days, with mean of 6.2 checks/day; (4) failed multiple-daily-injection therapy: A1C trajectory on optimized MDI (glargine + lispro) was 7.4 → 7.6 → 7.9 → 8.1 over the prior 12 months despite carbohydrate counting; documented 4 episodes of nocturnal hypoglycemia (BG <54 mg/dL by CGM) in past 6 months; (5) the patient meets CMS NCD 280.14 criteria for therapeutic CGM concurrently. Per Humana's Medical and Pharmacy Coverage Policy on Insulin Pumps and the ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026, insulin pump and CGM are recommended for T1DM with these clinical findings. Submitted within the 65-day window. Attached: C-peptide, DSME, BG log, A1C trend, hypoglycemia events.
citations: Humana Medical and Pharmacy Coverage Policy: External Insulin Infusion Pumps
CMS National Coverage Determination 280.14: Continuous Glucose Monitoring
ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026: Summary of Revisions

appeal_text: We request first-level Humana Medicare Advantage organization-determination reconsideration of the denial of CGM sensor (HCPCS A9276) for our patient with type 1 diabetes mellitus without complications (ICD-10 E10.9). The denial cited the split-channel sourcing requirement. Corrected ordering: the patient's pump (Tandem t:slim X2) was dispensed via DME provider on 2026-03-10; the CGM sensor (Dexcom G7) order is now redirected to the same DME provider per the Humana Diabetic Supply Policy Provider Letter (Eff. January 1, 2025). The corrected DME-channel order is attached. Substantive medical-necessity criteria are otherwise satisfied: (1) T1DM diagnosis; (2) the patient uses ≥3 daily insulin doses via pump; (3) in-person endocrinologist visit 2026-02-26 within 6 months of order, with documented intent to use CGM data for ongoing therapy adjustment; (4) 6-month follow-up scheduled 2026-08-26. Submitted within the 65-day window. Attached: DME corrected order, pump dispensing record, clinic visit note, follow-up schedule.
citations: Humana Diabetic Supply Policy — Provider Letter (Eff. January 1, 2025)
Humana Group Medicare Advantage Continuous Glucose Monitor Member Flyer (2026)
CMS National Coverage Determination 280.14: Continuous Glucose Monitoring

appeal_text: We request first-level Humana Medicare Advantage organization-determination reconsideration of the denial of CGM transmitter (HCPCS A9277) for our patient with type 2 diabetes mellitus without complications (ICD-10 E11.9). The denial cited basal-only insulin therapy. Corrected documentation: (1) regimen is now basal-bolus with glargine 30 units at bedtime and lispro 6 units pre-meal three times daily (4 total daily insulin administrations); (2) prescription record attached confirms lispro dispensed 2026-03-15 with 3-month supply; (3) the original PA submission was filed during the basal-only window and did not reflect the regimen transition documented in the 2026-04-01 clinic note; (4) the patient has had A1C 8.6 on basal-only therapy; (5) in-person clinic visit 2026-04-01 within 6 months of order, with 6-month follow-up scheduled. Per Humana's Group Medicare Advantage CGM Coverage Flyer (2026) and CMS NCD 280.14, multi-daily-injection (4+ daily) status is satisfied. Per ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026, CGM is recommended for patients on intensive insulin therapy. Submitted within the 65-day window. Attached: updated regimen, pharmacy fill records, A1C, clinic visit note.
citations: Humana Group Medicare Advantage Continuous Glucose Monitor Member Flyer (2026)
CMS National Coverage Determination 280.14: Continuous Glucose Monitoring
ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026: Summary of Revisions

appeal_text: We request first-level reconsideration of the denial of compounded semaglutide. We acknowledge Humana's coverage exclusion for compounded GLP-1s post-shortage resolution and concur with the policy. Our resubmitted request is for FDA-approved branded Wegovy (semaglutide 2.4 mg) for our patient with class 1 obesity (ICD-10 E66.811, BMI 33.4) and atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease (ICD-10 I25.10). Documentation: (1) BMI 33.4 confirmed 2026-04-30; (2) established ASCVD with prior NSTEMI 2024-05; (3) 12-week medically supervised intensive behavioral therapy 2026-01 through 2026-03 with insufficient weight loss; (4) registered dietitian visits documented; (5) HbA1C 5.7 (within prediabetic range, supporting cardiometabolic risk reduction strategy); (6) patient is on a Humana commercial group plan with anti-obesity GLP-1 coverage. The FDA-approved Wegovy labeling supports use for chronic weight management in patients with comorbid weight-related conditions and supports cardiovascular event-reduction benefit per SELECT trial. Per Humana's Anti-Obesity GLP-1 MCP for commercial plans, all clinical criteria are met for the FDA-approved product. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: BMI/weight trend, CV history, lifestyle-program records, dietitian notes, A1C.
citations: Humana Medical and Pharmacy Coverage Policy: Anti-Obesity GLP-1 Receptor Agonists
FDA-Approved Prescribing Information: Wegovy (semaglutide injection 2.4 mg)
FDA Guidance: Compounded GLP-1 Receptor Agonists

appeal_text: We request first-level Humana Medicare Advantage reconsideration of the denial of tirzepatide (Mounjaro). The denial cited concurrent dulaglutide therapy. Corrected pharmacy record: (1) dulaglutide (Trulicity) was discontinued 2026-03-12 per attached clinician order and pharmacy refill cancellation; (2) the 2026-03-28 Mounjaro initiation was 16 days after last dulaglutide dose (16-day washout consistent with dulaglutide's t1/2 of approximately 5 days, providing >3 half-lives of washout); (3) reason for switch: inadequate glycemic response on dulaglutide 4.5 mg weekly (A1C 7.8 unchanged from 7.9 over 24 weeks) and patient preference for tirzepatide's GIP/GLP-1 dual mechanism per ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026 elevated recommendation in patients with concurrent obesity and HFpEF; (4) the patient has documented HFpEF (LVEF 55%, NT-proBNP 540 pg/mL, NYHA II) — a phenotype now elevated in the 2026 ADA Standards Figure 9.4 to favor GIP/GLP-1 RA therapy. Per Humana's MCP on GLP-1 RAs, switch with documented washout is medically necessary. Submitted within the 65-day window. Attached: discontinuation order, refill cancellation, A1C trend, echo report, NT-proBNP.
citations: Humana Medical and Pharmacy Coverage Policy: GLP-1 Receptor Agonists for Type 2 Diabetes
ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026: Summary of Revisions
FDA-Approved Prescribing Information: Mounjaro (tirzepatide injection)
FDA-Approved Prescribing Information: Trulicity (dulaglutide)

appeal_text: We request first-level reconsideration of the denial of external insulin infusion pump (HCPCS E0784) for our patient with other specified diabetes mellitus without complications (ICD-10 E13.9), pancreatogenic diabetes following total pancreatectomy. The denial cited A1C 6.8% as inconsistent with conversion criteria. Reassessment supplements the conversion criterion: (1) the A1C of 6.8% masks profound glycemic lability documented on 14-day CGM 2026-04-08 through 2026-04-22: time-in-range (70-180) was 41%, time-below-range (<70) 18%, time-below-range (<54) 5%, time-above-range 41%, with coefficient of variation 47% (above 36% threshold defining glucose lability); (2) documented severe hypoglycemia events requiring third-party assistance: 3 episodes in past 12 months (attached EMS records and clinic notes); (3) total pancreatectomy 2024-02 with C-peptide < 0.1 ng/mL; (4) DSME completed; (5) failed optimized MDI regimen including basal-bolus with carbohydrate counting and pre-meal correction. Per Humana's MCP on External Insulin Infusion Pumps, glycemic lability and recurrent severe hypoglycemia satisfy the medical-necessity threshold regardless of A1C. Submitted within the 65-day window. Attached: CGM AGP report, hypoglycemia event log, surgical history, C-peptide, DSME documentation.
citations: Humana Medical and Pharmacy Coverage Policy: External Insulin Infusion Pumps
ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026: Summary of Revisions
AACE Clinical Practice Guideline: Use of Continuous Glucose Monitoring

appeal_text: We request first-level reconsideration of the denial of evolocumab (Repatha) for our patient with hyperlipidemia and clinical ASCVD (ICD-10 corrected to E78.00 and I25.10). Updated documentation: (1) clinical ASCVD with prior MI 2023-11; (2) statin ladder: rosuvastatin 40 mg daily (highest tolerated dose) 2024-01 through 2025-06 with LDL-C trajectory 142 → 118 → 110 mg/dL — above goal; documented intolerance to atorvastatin 80 mg (myalgia with CK 1.5× ULN, normalized after discontinuation), pravastatin 80 mg trial 12 weeks with statin-associated muscle symptoms; (3) ezetimibe 10 mg daily added 2025-06 through 2025-09, LDL-C decreased only modestly to 102 mg/dL; (4) most recent LDL-C 98 mg/dL, above ASCVD goal of <70 mg/dL per ADA/ACC/AHA guidance; (5) bempedoic acid 180 mg added 2025-09 through 2026-02, LDL-C 88 mg/dL, still above goal. Per Humana's MCP on PCSK9 Inhibitors, all step-therapy criteria are satisfied: clinical ASCVD, maximally tolerated statin, ezetimibe trial, persistent LDL-C above goal. FDA-approved Repatha labeling supports use in this population. Submitted within the 65-day window. Attached: LDL-C trend, statin trial records, ezetimibe records, bempedoic acid records.
citations: Humana Medical and Pharmacy Coverage Policy: PCSK9 Inhibitors
FDA-Approved Prescribing Information: Repatha (evolocumab)
ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026: Summary of Revisions

appeal_text: We request expedited first-level Humana Medicare Advantage organization-determination reconsideration of the denial of a hybrid closed-loop AID system upgrade for our pediatric patient (age 11) with type 1 diabetes mellitus with ketoacidosis without coma (ICD-10 E10.10). Supplemental documentation: (1) GAD-65 antibody positive (88 U/mL) and IA-2 antibody positive on 2026-02-19, confirming autoimmune type 1 diabetes; (2) C-peptide < 0.2 ng/mL; (3) family completed AID-system-specific training session on 2026-04-12 with both parents present and parent-system-management training acknowledgment attached; (4) the patient is currently using a Dexcom G7 CGM with documented ambulatory glucose profile and parents demonstrate competence; (5) recent DKA admission 2026-03-22 attributable to dosing miscalculation on MDI — AID system would prevent this failure mode; (6) Humana's MCP on Insulin Pumps and the ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026 recommend AID systems for children with T1DM. Per CMS-0057-F, the standard organization-determination decision must be issued within 7 calendar days; we request expedited determination given imminent DKA-recurrence risk on MDI. Submitted within the 65-day window. Attached: autoantibody panel, C-peptide, training acknowledgment, prior DKA admission summary, CGM AGP.
citations: Humana Medical and Pharmacy Coverage Policy: Insulin Pumps
ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026: Summary of Revisions
CMS-0057-F Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule (Eff. 2026-01-01)

appeal_text: We request first-level Humana Medicare Advantage reconsideration of the denial of dapagliflozin (Farxiga) for our patient with type 2 diabetes (ICD-10 E11.9) and stage 3 chronic kidney disease (ICD-10 N18.30, eGFR 38). Documentation: (1) A1C 8.0%; (2) eGFR 38 mL/min (within FDA-approved range for dapagliflozin); (3) metformin titrated to 1500 mg daily (renally-adjusted dose), unchanged glycemic response; (4) the patient is at elevated cardiorenal risk meeting the ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026 elevated-priority phenotype for SGLT-2 inhibitor therapy independent of A1C (Figure 9.4 elevates SGLT-2 in CKD and HF). Per Humana's MCP on SGLT-2 Inhibitors, coverage requires T2DM with documented metformin trial OR an elevated-priority phenotype (CKD, HF, or established ASCVD); both criteria are present. Submitted within the 65-day window. Attached: A1C, eGFR trend, metformin record.
citations: Humana Medical and Pharmacy Coverage Policy: SGLT-2 Inhibitors
ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026: Summary of Revisions
AACE Clinical Practice Guideline: Comprehensive Type 2 Diabetes Management Algorithm

appeal_text: We request first-level Humana Medicare Advantage reconsideration of the denial of tirzepatide (Zepbound) for our patient with severe obesity (BMI 41.2, ICD-10 E66.01) and moderate obstructive sleep apnea (ICD-10 G47.33, AHI 21). The denial cited Part-D obesity-indication exclusion. We submit that (a) per the December 2024 FDA approval of Zepbound for moderate-to-severe OSA in adults with obesity (SURMOUNT-OSA trial) and (b) per Humana's Group Medicare Advantage coverage for OSA-indicated Zepbound (covered under medical benefit when the OSA indication is established), the patient's denial reflects misclassification under the obesity-only Part-D exclusion. The patient's Zepbound prescription is for the FDA-approved OSA indication, not for chronic weight management alone. Documentation: (1) PSG 2026-03-08 reports AHI 21 (moderate OSA); (2) CPAP intolerance documented with adherence <2 hours/night over 60 days despite multiple mask trials and pressure adjustments; (3) BMI 41.2 confirmed; (4) ENT consult 2026-03-29 supports Zepbound for OSA with concurrent weight management goal. Submitted within the 65-day window. Attached: PSG, CPAP adherence report, ENT consult, BMI documentation, FDA Zepbound OSA-indication labeling.
citations: FDA-Approved Prescribing Information: Zepbound (tirzepatide injection)
Humana Medical and Pharmacy Coverage Policy: Anti-Obesity GLP-1 Receptor Agonists
